factors.name: US-Iran Hormuz Deal / Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz reopening agreement triggered sharp oil selloff (Brent to $80, WTI to $80 per Goldman/Morgan Stanley cuts). Lower energy costs reduce inflation near-term, which typically compresses long-duration tech multiples despite benefiting nominal growth.

factors.name: China Economic Deterioration
factors.summary: China retail sales fell -0.6% YoY in May (first contraction since pandemic), fixed asset investment -4.1% YTD (worst expectations), and house prices down -3.5% YoY. Weak domestic demand imperils Nasdaq-heavy exposure to Chinese discretionary and semiconductor demand.

factors.name: BOJ Rate Hike to 1% (as expected)
factors.summary: BOJ hiked 25bp to 1% (highest since 1995) and paused bond taper from April 2027—fully priced in markets. Deputy Governor Uchida's forward guidance is the real variable; yen pared gains post-announcement, signaling hike alone insufficient to support JPY or compress long-duration assets meaningfully.

factors.name: Real Yields / Long-Duration Tech Multiples
factors.summary: 2-year Treasury yield dropped to 4.02% post-Iran deal as rate hike bets trimmed. However, without a major cooler-than-expected inflation print or Fed pivot, nominal yield compression could be limited; tech multiple relief remains fragile ahead of Fed decision Wednesday.

factors.name: Fed Policy Decision Imminent (Wed 19-Jun)
factors.summary: FOMC decision Wednesday is the dominant near-term catalyst. Market is currently pricing no immediate rate cut, but Iran deal narrative (lower inflation/oil) could soften expectations; however, NO major inflation surprise released yet to force a re-pricing.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness / Risk-On Sentiment
factors.summary: USD Index holding near 99.70 but Iran deal sparked short-term risk-on rotation into EMs and commodities (AUD +0.37%, NZD +0.58% on Hormuz reopening). Weaker dollar typically bullish for US mega-caps with international revenue (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL).

factors.name: Tech Mega-Cap Earnings / AI Capex Outlook
factors.summary: No major earnings or forward guidance announcements in the news window. NVIDIA announced $20B+ debt sale (first since 2021), signaling capital deployment confidence, but no fresh order or demand commentary to move sentiment.

factors.name: Semiconductor Supply Chain
factors.summary: No TSMC, ASML, or Samsung-specific news. China's industrial output beat (+4.5% vs +4.2% expected) offers some support to chip demand, but retail sales miss and investment contraction temper growth optimism.

factors.name: Price Action Context (4h / 24h)
factors.summary: NAS100 flat on 4h (-0.04%), up only +0.37% in 24h. Move is minimal and unresolved; no breakout direction established yet, consistent with Phase 2 chop ahead of major event (Fed Wed).

key_levels.support: 30400–30450 (prior session lows, Iran deal bounce has not been sustained)
key_levels.resistance: 30650–30700 (early-week highs before Iran deal; next cap if risk-on accelerates)

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught in a volatile cross-current: the US-Iran Hormuz reopening deal initially sparked a relief rally (lower oil, weaker dollar, lower inflation expectations) beneficial to long-duration tech, but China's worst domestic demand print since the pandemic (retail sales first contraction since Covid) directly threatens semiconductor and discretionary-goods demand for Nasdaq-heavy mega-caps. The BOJ hike to 1% was fully priced and failed to sustain JPY gains, limiting the yen-carry unwind upside. With the Fed's decision hours away (Wed), markets are pricing a pause but remain sensitive to any inflation surprise that could force re-pricing; meanwhile, oil's sharp decline removes a key inflation floor, creating uncertainty over whether Treasury yields can durably compress. Price action is flat and unresolved—a Phase 2 chop that typically reflects indecision ahead of high-impact catalysts.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC Rate Decision & Powell Press Conference (Wed 19-Jun, ~18:00 GMT) — highest priority, expected hold but forward guidance on Fed path critical
US CPI (next print likely late-June, but PCE expectations will frame Fed path)
China economic follow-up (June stimulus, property sector intervention) — could reverse retail/investment deterioration narrative
Oil price stability / Hormuz reopening execution (next 1-2 weeks) — if pipeline fails or delays, energy inflation re-risks
NVIDIA, Microsoft, Apple, Google earnings (likely late June-early July) — hyperscaler capex guidance on cloud/AI demand essential
Treasury auction and real yield dynamics (next week) — will confirm whether Iran deal durably lowers real yields or is temporary
screening.risk_warning: If Fed signals hawkish hold (rates higher for longer) on Wed, or if Hormuz deal stalls / oil rebounds, tech multiples re-compress and risk-on unwinds. Conversely, if Fed hints at future rate cuts and China announces meaningful stimulus, NAS100 could accelerate higher. China's retail sales miss is a major wildcard—if it signals structural demand collapse rather than cyclical dip, Nasdaq exposure to China-sensitive sectors (TSLA, ASML, QCOM) could face downside pressure.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is unresolved and Phase 2; avoid chasing until after Fed decision (Wed). If NAS100 holds above 30,450, watch for breakout after FOMC; if it breaks 30,400, look for pullback entry toward 30,350 support before considering long positions. Short-term traders should wait for Fed clarity before size.

factors.name: Iran peace deal / oil deflation
factors.summary: US-Iran breakthrough eases geopolitical risk premium and energy inflation concerns (WTI at 3-month low ~$79). Lower energy costs reduce Fed rate-hike pressure, supporting long-duration growth equities. This is a multi-day tailwind for tech.

factors.name: Fed policy shift (Warsh hawkish preview)
factors.summary: BNY expects 'slight hawkish shift' in FOMC statement and dot plot under new Chair Kevin Warsh. FOMC decision due tomorrow (June 17), and market is pricing in potential tightening bias, which compresses long-duration growth multiples typical in NDX.

factors.name: Real yields and long-duration tech sensitivity
factors.summary: Oil/geopolitical relief reduces near-term inflation shock, but Fed hawkishness offsets dovish yield impact. Conflicting pressures leave real yield trajectory unclear heading into FOMC. Expect volatility in long-duration stocks (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, TSLA).

factors.name: SpaceX mega-IPO rally (single-name spillover risk)
factors.summary: SpaceX surging 11% premarket, valuation approaching $3T (exceeding Amazon). Elon Musk's $1T 2030 revenue target sparks Mag-7 enthusiasm and retail risk-on flow. Likely positive sentiment carryover to NDX, but concentrated in TSLA correlation and speculative positioning.

factors.name: China economic weakness
factors.summary: Retail sales posted first drop in 3+ years in May; urban investment contracted more than expected (per US Top News). Weakening China demand is structural headwind for semiconductor and capex outlooks (NVDA, ASML, semiconductor ETFs vulnerable).

factors.name: Semiconductor/chip capex narratives
factors.summary: STMicro raising $1.5B convertible bonds post AI rally; chip demand remains strong. However, China slowdown and potential Washington AI trade restrictions (Anthropic shutdown noted) create near-term headwind to capex forecasts and NVDA guidance.

factors.name: Central bank divergence (BoJ, RBA, BoK hawkish)
factors.summary: BoJ hiked to 1% (first since Dec, highest since 1995); RBA paused but flagged further tightening if inflation persists; BoK leaning hawkish. Global tightening bias offsets oil relief, limiting tech multiple expansion.

factors.name: USD strength despite oil weakness
factors.summary: Per ING, USD rallying on strong US data and Fed hawkish repricing despite oil decline. Strong dollar pressures international revenues of NDX mega-caps (~40-50% of earnings exposure).

factors.name: VIX/positioning into FOMC
factors.summary: No VIX data in news, but FOMC tomorrow means elevated gamma risk and potential chop in NDX into event. Options market likely pricing wider range into Fed announcement.

factors.name: Price action: 4h -0.04%, 24h +0.37%
factors.summary: Minimal intraday move despite major news (Iran, BoJ, China data, SpaceX). Suggests market is in consolidation/wait-and-see mode ahead of FOMC. No directional conviction yet.

key_levels.support: 30300 (overnight low pivot), 30000 (round number, May breakout zone)

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between two major cross-currents: the Iran peace deal and oil deflation (supportive for growth multiples and lower inflation expectations) versus new Fed Chair Warsh's hawkish policy bias and China economic deterioration (both bearish for long-duration tech earnings and capex). The SpaceX mega-IPO rally provides short-term sentiment lift, but the market is largely in consolidation mode awaiting tomorrow's FOMC decision. The 4h change of -0.04% and flat 24h move (+0.37%) suggest traders are sitting on hands into the Fed event; conviction is low.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision & dot plot (June 17, ~19:00 UTC, ~2 hours after close) — CRITICAL, likely hawkish surprise under Warsh
FOMC Chair Warsh press conference statement guidance
Continued US-Iran peace deal negotiations / Trump Hormuz reopening by Friday
China economic stimulus measures or policy response to May weakness
Major tech earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA guidance updates) — dates TBD, typically late June
Weekly jobless claims, any PCE inflation updates
Semiconductor supply chain commentary (TSMC, Samsung, ASML) on capex outlook
screening.risk_warning: FOMC hawkish surprise (higher terminal rate, slower cuts, tighter tone) would invalidate the oil-deflation bullish case and trigger sharp selloff in NDX, especially long-duration names (AAPL, NVDA, TSLA, MSFT). Conversely, if Warsh signals dovish pivot or Fed cuts return to table, oil relief combined with lower real yields could accelerate rally to 31000+. China stimulus announcement (fiscal or monetary) could also reverse negative capex narratives. Watch for single-name gamma squeezes in SpaceX, TSLA into FOMC.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 consolidation ahead of FOMC (highest-impact event in 24h). Avoid chasing; wait for either a FOMC-driven directional break (>0.5% move post-statement) to confirm next leg, or a retest of support 30300–30400 if sellers emerge. If bullish (dovish FOMC surprise), buy dip to 30400 with target 30800–31000. If bearish (hawkish FOMC), short any bounce to 30700 with target 30200. Do NOT size aggressively until volatility settles post-FOMC.
